title: austroblechnum durum
intro: Austroblechnum durum, also known as the Austral fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the Blechnaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antioxidant. The health benefits of Austroblechnum durum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory effects on wounds and skin irritations. Austroblechnum durum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves.
